prompt: Calling an overridable method from a constructor produces unexpected output. What is the bug?
choices: Calling an overridable method from a constructor dispatches to the subclass's override before the subclass fields are initialized; value is 0 (default), not 42
Derived.print() cannot access value because it is not yet allocated at Base() time
The constructor chain Base()→Derived() always initializes fields before any code runs
explanation: When new Derived() runs, Base() is called first. It calls print() which dispatches to Derived.print() polymorphically. But Derived's field initializers (value=42) haven't run yet, so value is the default int value 0. Output: 'Derived.print: 0'. This is a well-known Java pitfall.
